name: Grallariidae
description: Antpittas are plump, short-tailed, long-legged passerines that hop through the undergrowth of Neotropical forests. They are known for their loud, haunting songs and secretive behavior.

children.name: Grallaria
children.description: Grallaria is the largest genus of antpittas, containing medium to large species found primarily in the Andes.

children.name: Grallaria squamigera
children.description: The undulated antpitta is a large, striking antpitta with wavy black-and-buff barring across its underparts. It inhabits humid montane forests of the Andes from Colombia to Bolivia.

children.name: Grallaria ruficapilla
children.description: The chestnut-crowned antpitta has a distinctive bright chestnut cap and is found in the Andes of Colombia, Ecuador, and Peru. Its loud, trilling song is a characteristic sound of cloud forests.

children.name: Grallaria quitensis
children.description: The tawny antpitta is a uniform brownish-olive species restricted to high-altitude paramo and elfin forests of the northern Andes. It is one of the highest-elevation antpitta species.

children.name: Grallaria rufula
children.description: The rufous antpitta is a small, warm-rufous species found in Andean cloud forests from Venezuela to Bolivia. Recent genetic studies have split this species into several distinct cryptic species.

children.name: Grallaricula
children.description: Grallaricula is a genus of small antpittas, among the tiniest members of the family, found in humid montane forests.

children.name: Grallaricula flavirostris
children.description: The ochre-breasted antpitta is a tiny antpitta with a warm ochre breast and a narrow pale eye-ring. It ranges from Costa Rica to Bolivia in foothill and montane forests.
